name: Chicken-Fried Steak with Cream Gravy
recipeIngredient: 2 pounds round steak, tenderized by the butcher, cut
2 eggs, with 1 tbl. hot pepper sauce, beaten
recipeInstructions:
1. Season each steak on both sides with a pinch of salt and freshly ground black pepper, rubbing them into the meat.
2. One at a time, dredge the steaks in flour, then in egg mixture, then in cracker crumbs. Reserve 1/4 cup of the flour.
3. Warm corn oil in a large heavy skillet over medium heat. Add steaks and cook, turning carefully once or twice, until fully cooked through and crumb coating is brown and crisp, about 8 to 10 minutes total. Transfer to a platter and keep warm.
4. Pour off all but 4 tablespoons of the fat using a strainer and discard it. Return any remaining cracklings to the skillet and set over low heat.
5. Whisk reserved flour into the pan fat, cooking until smooth. Gradually whisk in milk. Raise heat slightly and bring gravy to a simmer.
6. Cook, stirring often and scraping brown deposits from skillet bottom, until gravy thickens, about 5 to 6 minutes. Adjust seasoning.
7. Arrange steaks on plates. Spoon mashed potatoes next to steaks and serve with gravy.
